data.epi_content:
As I lingered near any village, people would offer unwanted sacrifices out of fear.

If I secluded myself in the mountains, rumors would spread about haunted or cursed places, and village fools or children would come to test their courage.

Because of this, I'd grow tired of staying in one place and leave, always feeling like a wanderer.

At least having Evan by my side provided some comfort.

"Evan, should I get myself a new foot?"

"A foot? Why suddenly?"

"I feel bad always riding on Ivan, and for what I'm planning..."

I closed my mouth briefly, feeling embarrassed to say it out loud.

But then it seemed wrong to stop mid-sentence, so I continued with a straight face.

"I mean, to create a somewhat decent environment for raising a child, I shouldn't become known as the limping witch."

"If any child says something like that, we can temporarily move their family somewhere else."

Evan said this as if it were obvious.

But while that might work if it were just the two of us, I couldn't really agree with that approach.

"That's exactly what won't work."

Evan's eyebrow twitched slightly at my words.

Finally, I sighed and expressed my true feelings.

"Who would like a child of a witch?

Children need to be loved.

And forcing people to pretend to love them, whether through external pressure or coercion, is even worse."

Hearing this, Evan approached and kissed me.

Just a light touch of lips sharing warmth, then he pulled away and we continued walking.

"If that's the case. By the way, with me looking like this, won't it affect the child?"

"Acting normal and proper is the most basic quality of nobility.

We have to live while hiding how greedy and ugly we are beneath the surface.

Both you and I will be good at that. It's in our blood."

At those words, we both fell silent.

I kept quiet thinking about the ruins that had already disappeared, while Evan thought about his family that he had pushed aside.

Though this time, he hadn't completely destroyed them.

Snow had begun falling heavily.

I felt a chill in the foot that wasn't even there.

Feeling snowflakes melting on my nose, I tried to lighten the mood by moving closer to Evan and speaking softly.

"If we're going to settle somewhere, you'll need to take off those rags and dress properly.

I'll have to either attach a wooden decoration to my severed foot or mold some flesh into a strange-looking foot."

If I forcibly created a foot, it would move, but it would look quite frightening.

But if I covered it completely with socks and shoes, no one would care.

Still, I don't want to do it now, just a little... just for a moment, or perhaps when it's time to have and birth a child.

That would be a good time to make it. And I'd never take off my socks.

"Rags? This was a robe from the Imperial Palace, I'll have you know."

"Yes, but it's worn out from wearing only that."

At my words, Evan shrugged, took off the robe, and burned it in mid-air.

Then he pulled out a new robe from thin air and put it on.

"There, that looks much better."

"Of course, this is also from the Imperial Palace."
data.epi_content2:
"..."

I wanted to smack his forehead for looking so proud of himself.

Still, it did look much better now that it was clean.

Less bloody too.

"Anyway, about settling down.

I can't think of any place where children can laugh and play happily without any conflicts, where we could live quietly."

"If such a place existed, either you or I would have burned it down," Evan muttered under his breath.

I wanted to object, but thinking about it, I realized he was probably right, so I kept quiet.

"It would be better to have a child after settling down somewhere stable..."

At those words, I mocked Evan with a sneer.

When someone says something too correct, it can be quite irritating.

"Why, is someone offering to marry us?"

"Well, I've never heard of black magic users getting married."

"Probably because they all ran away.

Those who want to form families tend to despise danger, evil, and instability.

You and I could kill people on the street without thinking twice, but lately we've killed barely three creatures... almost none."

Well, after beating up some extremely vicious bandits who carried swords and kidnapped and murdered travelers, we hadn't killed any people.

Though I'm not sure if those bandits even qualified as human by normal standards.

"I don't want to do anything stupid that might provoke Vivian."

"Is that so?"

"Yes."

So for a while, instead of staying in one place, we continued wandering.

With each changing season, there was some novelty, but everywhere we went was familiar forest or tree-covered areas—places where people didn't live.

Ivan seemed to be suffering from some aftereffects of being tormented by children when he was an older dog, and he started limping badly.

I quickly compressed his brain stem to prevent pain and asked his slightly risen soul if he wanted to live longer, if he didn't want to die.

Ivan shook his head, barked a few times, licked my face once, and then dissipated.

Because of this, I went from being known as the limping witch who rode a dog to just a cripple.

I cut a suitable piece of wood to replace Ivan, made it into a walking stick, and limped around slowly.

I had nothing but time anyway.

Though I did feel a bit empty.

After wandering for quite some time, one day Evan handed me something wrapped in cloth and said:

"Wear this."

When I unwrapped it, inside was an intricately carved wooden foot.

"So that's what it was? You've been holding something all day, even using spells to hide it. I was wondering what it was."

"I was worried that if I made a foot myself, it might absorb magical power and develop consciousness... which I'd rather avoid."

A left foot that doesn't move as I want it to.

That sounds both ridiculous and extremely annoying.

If it were always underfoot—or rather, under my leg—staging protests and demonstrations, it would be a headache.

"...Anyway, try it on quickly."

When I placed the wooden foot model—which could hardly be called a prosthetic—against my blunt ankle, I immediately felt comfortable, as if it was supporting my ankle.

He must have used some kind of spell, but I didn't feel like asking about it.

"Nice. Comfortable."

At my words, Evan grumbled with dissatisfaction.
data.epi_content3:
"Is that all?"

I approached him, kissed his cheek once, and then stared at his face for a long time—his skin now snow-white and his eyes full of vitality from not doing harsh work lately—before lightly pressing my lips against his.

It started with just lips, but eventually, well, various meetings occurred.

Sometimes teeth accidentally met, or strange parts like the inside of cheeks or the roof of the tongue touched.

Both of us smelled strongly of the rough tobacco we had just shared.

"Is this enough to show my appreciation?"

"Still a bit lacking.

We should find a place to stay soon."

"I agree. Now that I have a foot and all."

It felt a bit uncomfortable where it touched my flesh, but despite the awkwardness, it was nice to feel like I had a foot again.

"What should we tell the child?

What kind of people should we say we are?"

"You're already worrying about that?"

"Well, even though we've been careful with timing, there's always a possibility."

"..."

As we continued walking, we finally spotted a village deep in the mountains.

As we approached, we saw it had a fence, but it was so low that you could step over it with just a slight lift of your leg, and there were no guards protecting the village.

Entering the village, we saw some children playing, men dragging hunted animals or skinning them, and women busy with various tasks.

They seemed to be farming, but it looked like they'd be lucky to grow potatoes or, if fortunate, a few turnips.

Well, at least a child wouldn't complain about side dishes living here.

"How about this place?"

"You said education environment was important too."

"I can teach the child myself."

"In that case, it's not bad.

By the way, do you know the language here?"

"Probably."

There were only four countries adjacent to the Empire, and I had learned all their languages when I was young.

Unlike my brother who went out to sea, I already liked this land where people lived.

Even knowing that capturing slaves was profitable, I didn't want to go near those filthy things.

"Staying in the village might be too much, so let's build a house near the mountain and visit occasionally.

If people come looking later, we can say we're noble children who fled from death threats, and they'll understand well enough."

In a vacant space adequately distant from the village, we quickly built a house using piles of soil, snow, and rocks.

I made an elegant roof, and Evan created everything else except the roof covering.

We made it about three times the size of the room we had at the Academy.

"By the way, what about furniture?"

"I brought most of it from the Imperial Palace."

I stared at Evan for a long time after hearing that.

At this rate, he might have even pulled out the palace pillars.

"Something doesn't feel right."

For some reason, I felt upset and stomped into the newly built house, slamming the door behind me.

I guess I was just being petty for no reason.
